seoTitle: Kerang East vs Kergunyah South — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Kerang East and Kergunyah South. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Kerang East vs Kergunyah South: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kerang East and Kergunyah South.
heading: Kerang East vs Kergunyah South — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kerang East, Victoria and Kergunyah South, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kergunyah South has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Kerang East (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kerang East or Kergunyah South safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kerang East has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Kergunyah South scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kerang East scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Kergunyah South scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kerang East or Kergunyah South?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kergunyah South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
